role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for selecting the home health agency and coordinating the nursing referral, whereas the Patient Navigator focuses on auditing patient demographics in Atlantis, educating the family on the medical assistant's role, and flagging transition gaps like provider no-shows to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: Elena Rodriguez, a 69-year-old recovering from a complex diabetic foot debridement, faced a critical risk to her recovery when the assigned home health nurse failed to arrive for the scheduled Day 1 dressing change. This service gap created immediate distress for her daughter, who was untrained in managing the specialized wound care required for Elena's heel. The Navigator acted as the vital communication link by documenting the staffing failure in Atlantis and immediately alerting the Social Worker to the lapse, ensuring the facility's clinical team could intervene while the family was directed to the appropriate professional channels for a resolution.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator performed a side-by-side audit of the facility face sheet and the Atlantis record; the patient's primary residence was verified, but a discrepancy was found in the zip code which the Navigator corrected in Atlantis to prevent a geographic staffing error by the home health provider.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with specialized wound care nursing and physical therapy support.
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator successfully saved the Healing Partners V-Card into the daughter's phone and reviewed the program flyer with her, emphasizing that the incoming caller ID would be the team coordinating the medical assistant's visit.
pre_dc_pulse_call: During the 24-hour pre-discharge call, the daughter confirmed that the home was prepared but expressed concern that she had not yet received a specific time window from the home health agency for the Saturday morning nursing visit.
atlantis_final_sync: The Navigator updated Atlantis with the confirmed Friday discharge date and scheduled the medical assistant's first visit for Saturday morning, contingent on the Social Worker's verbal confirmation that the home health agency was in place.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met with Elena and her daughter to explain the Healing Partners program; the daughter noted that her phone number in the facility records was outdated, so the Navigator updated the contact information in Atlantis.

The Social Worker informed the Navigator that the first choice for home health was unavailable due to staffing; the Navigator documented the delay in Atlantis and waited for the Social Worker to identify an alternative agency.

The Social Worker confirmed a new agency had accepted the referral for a Friday discharge; the Navigator updated the transition log in Atlantis and prepared the daughter for the Saturday morning medical assistant visit.

On Saturday morning, the daughter contacted the Navigator stating the home health nurse had not arrived by the scheduled 10:00 AM window; the Navigator documented the no-show in Atlantis and immediately notified the Social Worker via text.

format_2_triples.situation: The facility face sheet contained an outdated mobile number for the patient's daughter.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator updated the contact details in the Atlantis demographic portal to ensure seamless communication with the home team.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter expressed significant anxiety about how to change the diabetic wound dressing if the nurse was late.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator documented the family's sentiment and alerted the Social Worker that a bedside teaching session might be needed before discharge.

format_2_triples.situation: The home health nurse failed to appear for the first post-discharge visit.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator logged the service breakdown in Atlantis and instructed the family to contact the Social Worker for clinical instructions.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: After learning from the daughter that the nurse had not arrived, the Navigator documented the specific time of the call and the nature of the service gap in Atlantis, then waited for the Social Worker to respond to the initial notification.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary that the Social Worker owns the relationship with the vendor and is the only person authorized to resolve staffing failures with the agency.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator contacted the Social Worker directly to report the home health no-show and then spoke with the daughter to reinforce the medical assistant's arrival time, while directing her to call the agency's intake line for scheduling updates.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action keeps the Navigator within the lane of communication and documentation without attempting to perform the clinical or logistical duties of the Social Worker.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator called the home health agency's nursing supervisor to demand an immediate update on the nurse's location and then provided the daughter with instructions on how to temporarily reinforce the bandage until help arrived.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the Navigator is prohibited from contacting vendors directly and is not permitted to provide clinical advice or wound care instructions to the family.
